question: Explain two key benefits of using version control systems like Git in a software engineering project.
expectedPoints: Enables collaboration by allowing multiple developers to work on the same codebase simultaneously and merge changes.
Provides a complete history of changes, allowing developers to revert to previous versions and understand the evolution of the code.

question: Describe the purpose and key components of a Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document. Why is it a critical resource in project management?
expectedPoints: Serves as a formal agreement between clients and developers on what the software will do.
Key components include functional requirements, non-functional requirements, user interfaces, and system features.
It is critical for project management as it provides a baseline for planning, estimation, design, testing, and serves as a reference to avoid scope creep.

question: Compare and contrast the Waterfall and Agile project management methodologies. Discuss the scenarios for which each model is best suited, including their advantages and disadvantages.
expectedPoints: Waterfall is a linear, sequential approach best for projects with well-defined, stable requirements. Advantages: simple to manage, clear milestones. Disadvantages: inflexible to change, late testing.

Agile is an iterative, incremental approach best for projects with evolving or unclear requirements. Advantages: highly adaptable, delivers working software frequently. Disadvantages: can be less predictable, requires high customer involvement.
Conclusion on suitability: Waterfall for predictable projects (e.g., regulatory systems), Agile for innovative projects needing flexibility (e.g., consumer apps).
